scenes.narration: This is the Training app — the technician's surface for completing on-the-job training modules. It's separate from the main ERP so a tech can focus on learning without the rest of the business dashboard in the way.
Training is organized in layers. A standard operating procedure, or SOP, is the written reference. A training module wraps one or more SOPs with steps, a quiz, and optional supervisor sign-off.
The modules page shows every module available to you. Required modules are tagged in red. Each card shows estimated time and your progress status — not started, in progress, or completed.
Clicking a card opens the module detail. You see the description, any prerequisites, the full list of steps, and whether a quiz or supervisor sign-off is required at the end.

Each step is a self-contained lesson. A video, written instructions, the tools and materials you'll need, safety warnings, why it matters, key takeaways, and common mistakes. Mark it complete when you're ready to move on.
Take a private note against any step, or bookmark it for later reference. Your notes and bookmarks stay with you across sessions and devices.
After the last step, the module ends with a knowledge check. Multi-choice questions, a pass threshold — default eighty percent — and detailed feedback on every answer.
The My Progress page is your personal dashboard. Every module you've started or been assigned, with progress bars and completion dates. A good Monday-morning view of what's on your plate.
The Assignments tab separates active from past modules. If a due date is overdue, you see a red badge and expect a nudge from your trainer.

Trainers work on the other side of this system — from the ERP's Training section. They publish SOPs, build modules, and assign them to employees. The technician experience you just saw is driven by their work.
That's the Training app. See the Training operator manual for the full SOP, module, and assignment authoring flow trainers use every day.
